summary.intent: Implement task #288: a QrCode agnostic core component in ars-components (Rust, no_std) that renders a QR matrix supplied by the caller, including accessible labeling, error-correction level, sizing, colors, optional center overlay, and download trigger.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the stateless QrCode connect API mirroring the color_swatch pattern, with spec sync across core and adapter specs, 12 unit tests, 8 snapshots, 7 proptests, 1 doctest, and 1 spec-conformance test. All gates passed (cargo xci-fast, clippy, spec validate) and the PR was committed and pushed closing #288.

repo: ars-components is no_std by default (std is a feature, uses alloc) — this rules out generation crates living in the core layer without a feature gate.
color_swatch is the canonical stateless-specialized component analog: Api<'a> borrowing props, role='img' + aria-label, the real id pattern (if !props.id.is_empty()), no generate_id() helper.
The real id pattern across all components is `if !props.id.is_empty()` — generate_id() does not exist despite appearing in the spec.
Adapter specs explicitly own QR encoding via the qrcode crate in ars-dom; the agnostic core injects Option<QrMatrix> rather than generating it.

Proptests for stateless components live in tests/proptest_state_machines/specialized.rs and are marked #[ignore] for the nightly extended-proptest CI job — separator is a precedent for stateless components requiring them.
Snapshot orphan-check false positives occur for highlight when run without --features i18n; the correct command is --features i18n --unreferenced=reject.
cargo xci-fast is the pre-push gate (10 fast-profile checks including instrumented coverage rebuild, ~10-15 min).
Adapter sketches in spec/leptos-components and spec/dioxus-components are intentionally loose pseudo-code, consistent with the color_swatch sketch convention.
QrMatrix::get uses .unwrap_or(false) as a release-build defensive guard; debug_assert covers it in tests — leave it per the equivalent-defensive-guard guidance.
foreground/background/overlay_size props are stored in Props but not emitted as core attrs; they feed adapter-owned Pattern/Overlay rendering, matching the spec design.

code.finding: pixel_size formula: quiet_zone * 2 + modules * module_size — e.g. 44px = (3+4*2)*4 and 56px = (3+2*2)*8; returns 0.0 when no matrix is injected.
Api::new takes 4 args (props, matrix: Option<QrMatrix>, env, messages) — the spec originally had a 2-arg form calling a non-existent QrMatrix::generate, which was corrected in-task.
Module declarations are ordered alphabetically; qr_code (q) inserts after file_upload (f).
arb_value() must mix plain text and http/https URLs so the link-label URL branch is actually exercised; arb_matrix() builds square n×n grids so size == n holds.
Spec had two drift points vs the implementation: (1) Api::new called QrMatrix::generate which was never defined; (2) referenced generate_id() which doesn't exist. Both corrected in-task.

workflow: Post-implementation audit runs in 3 phases: Phase 1 (spec/impl drift), Phase 2 (missing wiring — catalog, feature gates, adapter refs, prelude, manifest), Phase 3 (coverage and test breadth). No findings deferred.
Spec sync is done in-task for the implementing PR — not deferred to a follow-up — so spec and impl match at merge time.
GitHub project board item moved to In Progress before writing code, not after.
Snapshots generated with --accept after a first compile-and-run confirms the tests pass; then reviewed manually before accepting.
cargo xci-fast run as background task with ScheduleWakeup fallback; notified on completion before committing.
Audit correctly distinguishes pre-existing warnings/orphans (doc warnings, highlight snapshots) from new regressions by checking file provenance.

friction: Spec drift in two places: Api::new called a non-existent QrMatrix::generate, and referenced generate_id() which doesn't exist — required explicit resolution and user confirmation before proceeding.
Audit Phase 3 initially missed proptests for the stateless QrCode component by incorrectly claiming stateless peers carry no proptests — user caught this; separator is a clear precedent.
Snapshot orphan check emits false positives for highlight feature-gated tests when run without --features i18n, requiring a second run with the feature flag to confirm clean state.
cargo xci-fast takes ~10-15 minutes, requiring a background task + wake-up mechanism rather than an inline await.
open_items: Adapter implementation for QrCode in ars-dom: actual QR matrix encoding via the qrcode crate is intentionally deferred to a future ars-dom adapter task.
foreground/background/overlay_size props are defined in Props but rendering is fully adapter-owned — no core attrs emitted; the adapter task must consume these.
118 pre-existing doc warnings across ars-components are unrelated to QrCode and were not addressed in this PR.
